title: tulipa orphanidea var. whittallii
intro: Tulipa orphanidea var. whittallii, also known as Whittall's tulip, is a rare, perennial species of flowering plant in the lily family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Tulipa orphanidea var. whittallii may include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as potential wound-healing properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are often dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
